title: A swapping scenario
wikitext:
[]: A swapping scenario             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Fri Oct 09, 2015 3:16 pm Okay, so shortly after Khara was founded, production on NTE began. But for this scenario Anno and Tsurumaki switch roles. What do you suppose the result would be of having EVA seen primarily through Kazuya's eyes?

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Sat Oct 10, 2015 12:11 am Who knows? It might even be better, with Tsurumaki being more able to celebrate the series and less concerned with "rehabilitating" it.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Oct 10, 2015 3:40 am Compiling_Autumn wrote: Who knows? It might even be better, with Tsurumaki being more able to celebrate the series and less concerned with "rehabilitating" it.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Oct 10, 2015 9:07 am A celebration that's 4 movies long (or even 1 movie long, for that matter) would just be tiresome to watch. We all know that the best narrative celebrations of other narratives are all done in homes or bit-part cameos anyway, so the best thing would be a fight scene that was choreographed kind of like Eva's fight scenes, but in a series that had nothing to do with Evangelion whatsoever. In this sense, Anno's active changing up of a familiar story is far more entertaining as far as recognizing Evangelion is concerned. But that was my thought, and not Tsurumaki's thought. I've no idea what he'd do. He's done so little lead directorial work, that the only way I can identify him is in the craziness of his derivative works.

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Sat Oct 10, 2015 7:19 pm ^Well said. I'm very glad the movies aren't just Death and Rebirth: Expanded. If Tsurumaki had been the one in charge, then the first half of 1.0 might not have been so slavishly devoted to episodes 1 and 2. Who knows where it might have gone from there?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Oct 10, 2015 7:26 pm Sachi wrote: Anno's take sounds much more interesting. What could be better about doing more of the same?

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Sat Oct 10, 2015 11:22 pm Okay, there's a difference between celebrating something and copying it. For example, Diebuster as a "celebration" of Gunbuster. To clarify, the Tsurumaki Rebuild would either be one of the greatest things he had ever done, or it would be ridiculous, too much like Gurren Lagann, but still pretty good. Just like how the actual Rebuild Series has some questionable changes but is still pretty good.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Oct 11, 2015 6:51 am ^ Diebuster was also a gap-filler for Gunbuster. Granted, it was one that I didn't think would work until I actually saw it, but it won me over anyway. So maybe Tsurumaki could have actually followed up Evangelion with something successfully celebratory, but I really doubt it. Gun buster was all about celebration of other works to begin with, so a sequel celebrating a celebratory narrative work was fun in its own meta way. Evangelion, on the other hand, didn't really celebrate anything when it referenced older material. Rather, Evangelion questioned everything by referencing older material. And since Tsurumaki is the kind of director that he is, the only thing I can see him doing to celebrate Evangelion's questioning existence would be to parody it in some way. Parodies are great and all, I enjoy things that parody Evangelion all the time, but to have a parody be the main feature in an Evangelion franchise just seems wrong to me. Chuckman wrote: Jury's still out, fourth film is not in. My sophisticated mathemagical techniques forecast a 40% chance that FINAL will cause NTE to disappear up its own ass in an orgy of weird nerd guilt and incoherent moral philosophy. Or it'll be awesome. There is no middle ground.

But for this scenario Anno and Tsurumaki switch roles. What do you suppose the result would be of having EVA seen primarily through Kazuya's eyes?

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Sat Oct 10, 2015 12:11 am
